overview.summary: Sorting arranges elements in a specific order, creating predictability.
learningObjectives: Understand O(N log N) sorting behavior
Recognize when sorting simplifies a problem
overview.interviewRelevance: Often the critical first step that turns an O(N^2) naive solution into an O(N log N) elegant one.

paragraphs: Whether it's displaying e-commerce products by lowest price, ranking search results, or ordering an airline seating chart, sorting brings order.
Most languages have optimized highly-tuned hybrid sorts (like Timsort) built-in.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Once data is sorted, finding duplicates, binary searching, and identifying near numbers becomes trivial O(N) or O(1).

definition.technicalDefinition: Algorithm to arrange elements of a list in a certain order (typically numerical or lexicographical).

vocabulary.definition: A sort that requires O(1) extra space.
A sort that preserves the relative order of equal elements.
Recursively breaking a sequence down, sorting parts, and combining them.

keyProperties: Optimal comparison sorts take O(N log N) time.
Non-comparison sorts (Counting, Radix) can achieve O(N).
Most library sorts are O(N log N) and stable.

operations.name: Merge Sort
operations.description: Divide the array in half recursively, then merge sorted halves.
operations.visualNote: Array breaks down into 1-element chunks, then combines gracefully.

operations.name: Quick Sort
operations.description: Pick a pivot, partition smaller to left and larger to right, recurse.

code.python:
def quick_sort(arr):
    if len(arr) <= 1: return arr
    pivot = arr[len(arr) // 2]
    # ... recursion ...

operations.visualNote: Pivots snap into their final global positions early on.

practice.title: Merge Intervals
practice.keyInsight: Sorting by start time naturally groups overlapping intervals.

practice.title: Top K Frequent Elements
practice.keyInsight: Sort the frequency map counts, or use Bucket Sort in O(N).

practice.title: Sort Colors
practice.keyInsight: Dutch National Flag problem, sorting in O(N) via swapping 3 categories.

practice.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
practice.keyInsight: Quickselect conceptually sorts only the half containing K.

practice.title: Contains Duplicate
practice.keyInsight: Sorting makes duplicates sit directly next to each other.

practice.title: Two Sum II - Input array is sorted
practice.keyInsight: Sorting enables the fast two-pointer technique.
